Title: Evolution of a flipped pathway creates metabolic innovation in tomato trichomes through BAHD enzyme promiscuity

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Sl-ASAT2 promoter (trichome-specific, type I/IV); co-expressed with Sp-ASAT3 under Sl-ASAT3 promoter in transgenic cultivated tomato; promoter type: inducible/tissue-specific
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: M82; BIL6180 background for RNAi validation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering trichome acylsugar structural diversity and potentially pest-protective acylsucrose blends by stacking with Sp-ASAT3. Deployment should target glandular trichomes using native trichome-specific promoters to avoid ectopic metabolism. Best used with pathway-context matching because product outcome depends on epistasis and reaction order with ASAT3.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to the pyranose-ring R3 position of the diacylsucrose intermediate S2:10 (iC5R2, iC5R4), forming P-type triacylsucroses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of P-type triacylsucroses with all three acyl chains on the pyranose ring when combined with Sp-ASAT3; sufficient together with Sp-ASAT3 to create P-type acylsucroses in cultivated tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic co-expression in S. lycopersicum M82; purified recombinant enzyme assays; LC-ToF-MS metabolite profiling; chromatographic co-elution with in vivo products; site-directed mutagenesis; RNAi pathway support via Sp-ASAT3 silencing context.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown in growth chamber at 28°C light/20°C dark; trichome context is critical; no special external treatment reported.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in type I/IV glandular trichomes when driven by Sl-ASAT2 promoter in transgenic S. lycopersicum.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native ASAT genes discussed as glandular trichome-expressed in tomato species.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream; third step in the flipped P-type pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genetic screening of 257 BILs by LC-ToF-MS metabolite profiling; sequence data in GenBank KT359557 and related ASAT2 accessions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its function differs from Sl-ASAT2 by reversed pathway order. Sp-ASAT2 does not use monoacylsucrose S1:5 as substrate, but uses the Sp-ASAT3 product S2:10 (iC5R2, iC5R4). Activity is part of an epistatic two-locus innovation with Sp-ASAT3.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No growth or yield penalty reported; engineering outcome is context-dependent and may generate mixed F- and P-type acylsucroses in cultivated tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Diacylsucrose S2:10 (iC5R2, iC5R4); acyl-CoA donors including iC5-CoA and nC12-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-defining step in combination with Sp-ASAT3; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sp-ASAT2 is one of two S. pennellii loci required for P-type acylsucrose accumulation. 2. It acylates the Sp-ASAT3-made diacylsucrose intermediate at pyranose R3 to produce P-type triacylsucroses. 3. Together with Sp-ASAT3 it is necessary and sufficient to reconstitute the flipped pathway in cultivated tomato. 4. A small number of amino acid changes alter substrate preference and pathway behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against herbivores and fungal pathogens; natural pest control metabolite

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Sl-ASAT3 promoter (trichome-specific, type I/IV); co-expressed with Sp-ASAT2 under Sl-ASAT2 promoter in transgenic cultivated tomato; promoter type: inducible/tissue-specific
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value enzyme for rewiring acylsugar pathway order and generating new acylsucrose chemotypes. Should be stacked with Sp-ASAT2 and expressed specifically in trichomes. Because its product is a noncanonical intermediate not accepted by cultivated tomato ASATs, engineering must include the compatible downstream enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to monoacylsucrose S1:5 at the pyranose-ring R2 position, forming a distinct diacylsucrose intermediate used in the P-type pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates the flipped pathway that leads to P-type acylsucrose accumulation; required for second step of S. pennellii/BIL6180 pathway and sufficient with Sp-ASAT2 to produce P-type acylsucroses in cultivated tomato.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic co-expression in S. lycopersicum M82; recombinant enzyme assays; NMR of enzymatic product; HPLC retention-time comparison with Sl-ASAT2 product; RNAi silencing in BIL6180; LC-MS metabolite profiling; site-directed mutagenesis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Plants grown in controlled chamber conditions; trichome cell context is essential; no special exogenous treatment reported.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in type I/IV glandular trichomes when driven by Sl-ASAT3 promoter in transgenic S. lycopersicum; silencing in BIL6180 reduces acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native ASAT genes discussed as glandular trichome-expressed in tomato species.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream; second step in the flipped P-type pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-ToF-MS metabolite profiling; sequence data in GenBank KM516151 and related ASAT3 accessions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Sl-ASAT3, Sp-ASAT3 uses monoacylsucrose rather than diacylsucrose as acceptor, reversing ASAT2/ASAT3 reaction order. RNAi silencing strongly reduces total acylsugars and no diacylsucrose accumulates detectably.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No explicit fitness penalty reported; in cultivated tomato co-expression yields mixed F- and P-type products due to coexistence with endogenous F-type pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Monoacylsucrose S1:5 (iC5R4); acyl-CoA donor such as iC5-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch enzyme and decisive initiator of flipped pathway; not explicitly called rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sp-ASAT3 catalyzes the second step of the S. pennellii P-type pathway by using monoacylsucrose as acceptor. 2. Its product is a pyranose-acylated diacylsucrose distinct from the cultivated tomato intermediate. 3. Silencing Sp-ASAT3 in BIL6180 greatly reduces acylsugars, validating its in vivo necessity. 4. Together with Sp-ASAT2 it flips reaction order relative to cultivated tomato and creates metabolic innovation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against herbivores and fungal pathogens; natural pest control metabolite

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native endogenous expression in cultivated tomato; Sl-ASAT2 promoter used in transgenic construct to drive Sp-ASAT2; promoter type: trichome-specific/tissue-specific
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be engineered by targeted amino acid substitution (e.g., C304G) to broaden substrate acceptance and create P-type activity while retaining F-type activity. Useful as a chassis enzyme for synthetic diversification of acylsugars.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: In cultivated tomato, transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to monoacylsucrose S1:5 at pyranose-ring R3 to form an F-type diacylsucrose intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports F-type acylsucrose biosynthesis; wild-type enzyme does not accept the Sp-ASAT3-made P-type diacylsucrose intermediate, but mutant C304G gains P-type activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Published pathway reference integrated with comparative in vitro assays; site-directed mutagenesis; recombinant protein assays; LC-MS/HPLC comparison.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Glandular trichome-expressed in cultivated tomato; promoter used as trichome-specific promoter in transgenics.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream; second step in cultivated tomato F-type pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank KT359567; LC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic sequence comparison across tomato clade.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single substitution C304G is sufficient to confer ability to use S2:10 (iC5R2, iC5R4), making the enzyme more promiscuous while retaining native F-type activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering increased promiscuity may generate mixed pathway outputs and altered acylsugar composition; no whole-plant penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: F-type diacylsucrose S2:10 (acylated at R3 and R4)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-defining pathway step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sl-ASAT2 performs the second step of the cultivated tomato F-type acylsucrose pathway. 2. It uses monoacylsucrose S1:5, unlike Sp-ASAT2. 3. Mutation C304G confers P-type substrate acceptance without eliminating F-type activity, highlighting enzyme promiscuity in pathway evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against herbivores and fungal pathogens; natural pest control metabolite

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native endogenous expression in cultivated tomato; Sl-ASAT3 promoter used in transgenic construct to drive Sp-ASAT3; promoter type: trichome-specific/tissue-specific
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for protein engineering to redirect sucrose acylation position and pathway order. Triple substitution Y161H/C162S/T289V confers P-type activity, suggesting design rules for BAHD enzyme engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: In cultivated tomato, transfers an acyl group from acyl-CoA to the furanose ring of a diacylsucrose intermediate, producing F-type triacylsucrose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports F-type acylsucrose biosynthesis in cultivated tomato; triple mutant gains ability to acylate monoacylsucrose in a P-type manner while retaining some F-type activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparative recombinant enzyme assays; site-directed mutagenesis; LC-MS/HPLC product analysis; phylogenetic comparison.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor to F-type higher acylsucroses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Glandular trichome-expressed in cultivated tomato; promoter used as trichome-specific promoter in transgenics.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream; third step in cultivated tomato F-type pathway
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: GenBank KM516150; LC-MS metabolite profiling; phylogenetic sequence comparison across tomato clade.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Triple mutant Y161H/C162S/T289V confers P-type-like monoacylsucrose acylation; residues V353L and S380HP in reciprocal Sp-ASAT3 mutagenesis relate to F-type activity. Demonstrates multi-residue control of acceptor specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Engineering may create promiscuous enzyme with mixed F- and P-type activities; no whole-plant penalty reported.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Diacylsucrose S2:10 (aiC5R3, iC5R4); acyl-CoA donors including iC5-CoA and in some orthologs nC12-CoA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-defining step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Sl-ASAT3 catalyzes the third step of cultivated tomato F-type acylsucrose biosynthesis using a diacylsucrose acceptor. 2. It contrasts with Sp-ASAT3, which acts earlier on monoacylsucrose. 3. Three amino acid substitutions can switch Sl-ASAT3 toward P-type activity, showing how small sequence changes enabled pathway flipping during evolution.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protection against herbivores and fungal pathogens; natural pest control metabolite
